title: stipa oligotricha subsp. kiemii
intro: Stipa oligotricha subsp. kiemii, also known as Kiem's little barley-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Kiem's little barley-grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Kiem's little barley-grass can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
